methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $8.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $5.6B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Together AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $3.3B

data.narrative:
Reflection AI is a Series A artificial intelligence and software company founded by Misha Laskin and Ioannis Antonoglou, currently valued at $8.0B post-money after raising $2.0B. The peer universe comprises 9 comparable companies across AI & Software, including 1 public company (Palantir) and 8 private firms. Adjacent-stage comparables include Physical Intelligence ($5.6B, Series B), Together AI ($3.3B, Series A), and Black Forest Labs ($3.2B, Series B).

Two valuation methodologies were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies an enterprise value of $16.0B. Peer-median analysis across the three closest comparables in sector and stage yields $3.3B, with a range of $3.2B to $5.6B. The substantial divergence reflects differences in fundraising velocity and public market sentiment toward AI infrastructure versus downstream applications.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $2.8B (low) to $18.4B (high), with a median of $9.7B. The current $8.0B valuation sits near the triangulated center, suggesting fair pricing conditional on execution risk. Key uncertainties include capital intensity of model development, durability of competitive advantage in a capital-abundant segment, and macro sentiment toward AI funding. The wide interquartile range underscores sensitivity to which comparable set is deemed most relevant.
